note: Commentary and evaluative labels removed from raw data. This content is interpretive and belongs in analysis, not in raw data inputs.

removed_items.original_value: Since 2020, government forces social media companies to maintain local offices and comply with takedown demands; companies refusing face fines and ad bans
90% media ownership concentrated in pro-government groups (RSF 2025); foreign online media required to obtain local licenses since 2019
EVALUATIVE STRIPPED: 'low'
CPJ reports 11 journalists imprisoned as of Dec 2024; Bianet reported 57 journalists detained and 36 convicted in 2024; RTÜK revoked license of Açık Radio July 2024

Internet penetration from DataReportal 2025 (77.3M users, 88.3%); social media 66.7% of population; Free Web Turkey reported ~14,000 online news articles blocked in 2023 plus 197,000 websites; 2022 disinformation law mandates up to 3 years prison

GDP/capita and PPP updated to IMF 2026 projections; Gini pretax updated to TÜİK 2024 (0.448); youth unemployment from Wikipedia/Economy of Turkey (16.3%); AROPE 30.4% (2024); social welfare spending 10.4% of GDP (OECD); national poverty rate 13.0% (World Bank 2024)
Large positive gap: GDP >> GNI signals significant foreign capital extraction. Domestic production far exceeds what residents actually earn.
Voter turnout 87.04% in 2023 presidential first round (84.15% second round); Turkey ranked second-lowest in media literacy in Europe per 2018 study
No constitutional right to internet; no net neutrality law; biometric data covered under KVKK (data protection law); right to be forgotten recognized via court rulings
EVALUATIVE STRIPPED: 'extensive'
2017 constitutional referendum expanded presidential powers significantly; 7% electoral threshold; president appoints constitutional court members

Healthcare: 75.2% public funding, 16.5% out-of-pocket, 24.8% private (2023); hospital beds 3.05/1,000 (World Bank 2023); healthcare per capita $548 (World Bank 2023); primary enrollment 102.4% gross (World Bank 2023); NEET rate 22.9% (World Bank 2024); compulsory education 12 years; private tutoring widespread but unquantified
polarized — strong within groups, near-zero bridging capital
No recent WVS wave for Turkey; last available 2011. Estimate: low (15-20%) based on regional patterns.
one of highest rates in Europe
Maternal mortality 15/100,000 (World Bank 2023); divorce rate 2.0/1,000 population (Eurostat 2023); hospital beds 3.05/1,000 (World Bank 2023)
Four national confederations: TÜRK-İŞ (1.75M), DİSK (327K), HAK-İŞ (340K), KESK (300K); total ~2.7M members across all unions
EVALUATIVE STRIPPED: 'severe'
EVALUATIVE STRIPPED: 'nominal'
low — strong family/community networks
high — communities enforce conformity
Birth rate 11.0/1,000 (World Bank 2024); suicide rate 2.69/100,000 age-standardized (WHO 2021 via World Bank)
Top 10% income share 34.4% (World Bank 2023); bottom 20% income share 5.5% (World Bank 2023); Bottom 50% living below median: 16.3% (World Bank 2023); Gini pretax 0.448 (TÜİK 2024)

Tax-to-GDP ratio 41.65% (2021); individual income tax 15-35% (not 40%); corporate tax flat 20%; VAT standard 20% (reduced rates 1%, 8%, 10%); capital gains included in ordinary income tax for individuals

Education spending 3.1% GDP (World Bank 2022); R&D 1.42% GDP (World Bank 2023); healthcare 6.3% GDP (OECD/Wikipedia — lowest among OECD); military 1.72% GDP (World Bank 2023); social protection 10.4% GDP (OECD SOCX)
high — Western Turkey vs. Southeast
severe — thousands
effectively none post-2016
